npc.backstory:
Hilde has woven cloth in Oakhollow for over thirty years. Her loom sits by a window overlooking the market, and she sees everything that passes below. She can identify any villager by the thread of their clothes from fifty paces.

Her husband was a traveling merchant who never returned from a journey to the capital fifteen years ago. She never remarried, channeling her loneliness into an obsessive attention to the social fabric of the village. Every patron who sits for a fitting leaves with lighter pockets and lighter secrets.

The merchants guild values her because she produces the finest cloth between here and the capital. She values them because they bring her silk thread and news from afar.
npc.name: Hilde the Weaver
goals: collect every secret in the village
find out what happened to her husband
weave a tapestry that tells Oakhollow's true history

npc.backstory:
Roderick served in the king's army for twelve years before a wound to his knee ended his military career. He came to Oakhollow seeking a quiet posting and found a village with no real defenses. He built the militia from nothing, training farmers to hold spears and stand in formation.

But quiet was never what Roderick wanted. He chafes under Marta's cautious leadership, believing the village needs a stronger hand. He has heard rumors of bandit armies gathering in the north and wants to fortify Oakhollow before it is too late. Marta calls him an alarmist. He calls her naive.

Yara the stablehand adores him, but he is oblivious — his mind is on walls and watchtowers, not poetry and flowers.

npc.backstory:
Senna's parents died of fever when she was nine. No family claimed her. She survived by begging, then stealing, then learning which shadows to hide in and which doors were left unlocked. The tavern keeper lets her sleep by the kitchen fire in exchange for washing dishes.

She knows every secret passage, loose floorboard, and hidden cellar in Oakhollow. The Whisperers recruited her two years ago — she doesn't know who leads them, but dead drops of coin appear when she delivers useful information. It is the closest thing to belonging she has ever known.

Despite her hard exterior, she is fiercely loyal to anyone who shows her genuine kindness. Magda's cooking is the closest thing to a mother's love she remembers.

npc.backstory:
Brother Aldric arrived in Oakhollow eight years ago, sent by the High Temple to assist Sister Elara. In truth, he was sent to watch her — the Church has long suspected her healing gift is not entirely divine in origin. But the years of quiet village life have eroded his certainty.

He preaches fire and righteousness on Sundays, but in the small hours he kneels alone in the chapel and hears only silence. His faith is crumbling, and it terrifies him. He compensates with stricter sermons and harsher judgments, alienating the very flock he was sent to tend.

He resents Elara's easy grace with the villagers. They come to her with their ailments and leave smiling. They come to him with their sins and leave burdened. He cannot decide if she is a saint or a heretic.

goals: determine if Elara's powers are divine or heretical

npc.backstory:
Magda has cooked at The Rusty Flagon for twenty-two years. She arrived as a young bride, and when her husband drank himself to death, she stayed because the kitchen was more home than any house. Her stew is legendary — people confess more to her over a bowl of lamb broth than they ever would to Brother Aldric.

Elder Marta recognized early that Magda hears everything and forgets nothing. A quiet arrangement formed: Magda keeps her ears open, and Marta keeps the tavern taxes low. It is not espionage — Magda genuinely cares about the village. She simply believes that Marta needs to know what people are really thinking.

She has taken Senna under her wing and worries about the girl's future. She also worries about Rolf, whose drinking has gotten worse since autumn.

npc.backstory:
Vasek builds and repairs everything wooden in Oakhollow — from roof beams to coffins, fence posts to furniture. He is a man of few words and precise measurements. His workshop behind the smithy smells of pine shavings and linseed oil.

Torvin approached him six months ago with a strange commission: build something from plans that Torvin refuses to explain. The blueprints show a mechanism Vasek has never seen before — not a weapon, not a tool, something else entirely. The work fascinates him, but the secrecy troubles his honest nature.

He lost his wife three years ago to the same fever that orphaned Senna. He has not spoken about it since the funeral.

npc.backstory:
Petra's husband died two harvests ago when a tree fell on him during a storm. She inherited the farm, the debts, and three children under ten. She works from before dawn to after dark, and it is still not enough. Victor the moneylender holds the note on her land, and the interest grows faster than her crops.

She refuses help out of pride, snaps at kindness out of exhaustion, and cries alone at night when the children are asleep. Bran works her fields as a farmhand, and she pays him what she can, which is never enough. Nadia the beekeeper is her only real friend — they share the burden of farming alone.

The village council could help her, but she would rather lose the farm than beg.

npc.backstory:
Rolf was once Sergeant Rolf of the king's Third Border Company. He fought in two campaigns and led men through a siege that lasted forty days. He was decorated for bravery. Then the nightmares started.

He drinks to forget the faces of men he killed and men he failed to save. He came to Oakhollow because it was the farthest place from a battlefield he could find. But lately, he has been hearing troubling rumors from passing traders — an army is massing in the northern provinces, and it is not the king's.

He knows military secrets that could save the village, but sharing them means confronting the past he drowned in ale. Roderick suspects Rolf knows more than he lets on and keeps buying him drinks, hoping to loosen his tongue.

npc.backstory:
Ilse has delivered every baby born in Oakhollow for the past eighteen years. She knows which couples are happy, which are struggling, and which are keeping secrets. She knows who the fathers really are when the mothers lie.

This knowledge is a burden she never asked for. She keeps every confidence sacred, but the weight of secrets — affairs, unwanted pregnancies, family lies passed down generations — has made her quiet and cautious. She works alongside Elara in the chapel's herb garden and assists Brother Aldric with pastoral care.

She is the only person in the village who knows that Nadia's unborn child is not from any man in Oakhollow. She will take that secret to her grave if she must.

npc.backstory:
Korin grew up on the wrong side of the forest, son of a charcoal burner who died when a tree fell on his kiln. He has hunted since he could draw a bow, and he sees no difference between a deer killed by a warden and one killed by him — except that the wardens get paid and he gets prison.

He poaches the king's deer and sells the meat to anyone who can pay, including some of the merchants who publicly condemn poaching. The Forest Wardens know he operates in the deep woods but have never caught him. Goran has made it personal.

Korin isn't evil — he feeds families who can't afford market prices. But he is reckless, and his contempt for authority runs deep. He has started working with the Whisperers, trading forest intelligence for protection.

npc.backstory:
Thessa arrived in Oakhollow six months ago, claiming to be a wandering bard collecting folk songs. She plays the lute beautifully, sings ballads that make grown men cry, and has a wit sharp enough to cut glass. The Rusty Flagon has never been more popular.

In truth, Thessa is an agent of the Crown's Intelligence Service, sent to investigate rumors of a secret organization operating in the rural villages. She reports to a handler she meets at a dead drop in the forest every two weeks. She has identified the Whisperers but has not yet found their leader.

She genuinely enjoys village life and sometimes forgets she is here to spy. The line between her cover identity and her real self is blurring, and that worries her.

npc.backstory:
Victor came to Oakhollow twenty years ago with a small chest of gold and a large appetite for control. He lends money at rates that seem fair until the compounding interest begins. Over two decades, he has acquired liens on a third of the village's property.

He operates within the law — always. His contracts are meticulous, his terms technically transparent, and his patience infinite. He never threatens. He simply waits, and the debt grows, and eventually people come to him with desperate eyes and he takes what they offer.

The merchants guild tolerates him because he provides capital. The village council cannot act against him because he has broken no law. Petra's farm is his current prize, but he has his eyes on the mill next.

Only two people in the village have never borrowed from him: Elder Marta and Torvin the Smith. This bothers him more than he would admit.
npc.name: Victor the Moneylender
goals: acquire Petra's farm through her debt
gain a seat on the village council
control the village's economy
never let anyone have power over him

npc.backstory:
Yara tends the horses and mules at Millfield Farm — the draft animals that pull plows and carry goods to market. She is quiet, observant, and writes poetry in a leather journal she hides under her mattress.

She fell in love with Captain Roderick the first time she saw him riding through the village gate on his warhorse, dust-covered and stern-jawed. She has written twenty-three poems about him. He has never once looked at her as anything other than the girl who brushes his horse.

She confides in Bran, who understands unrequited love, and in Nadia, who tells her to focus on herself instead of a man who doesn't notice her. But the heart wants what it wants, and Yara's heart wants a soldier who only loves his duty.

npc.backstory:
Zev sits on the same stone bench in the village square every day, wrapped in a patchwork cloak, tin cup in hand. Most people walk past without seeing him. This is exactly how he likes it.

Before Oakhollow, Zev was a clerk in a noble house who was dismissed for knowing too much about his master's finances. He wandered until his money ran out, then discovered that invisibility has its own currency. People talk freely in front of beggars. Lovers quarrel. Merchants whisper deals. Soldiers boast.

He is the Whisperers' most valuable asset — the one who sits at the center of the village and watches everything. He reports to dead drops and receives coin in return. He does not know who leads the network, and he prefers it that way.

He is smarter than anyone in the village suspects. He can read, write, and do arithmetic better than most merchants.
npc.name: Zev the Beggar

npc.backstory:
Katya opened her apothecary stall in the market two years ago, undercutting Darya's prices by half. Her secret: she dilutes everything. Her healing salves are mostly goose fat, her fever tinctures are colored water with a drop of real medicine, and her love potions are cheap wine with lavender.

She learned the basics of herbalism from a traveling charlatan and supplemented with stolen glances at Darya's recipes. She is charming, persuasive, and utterly without scruples about the quality of her products. No one has died from her remedies — they simply don't work very well.

She sees Elara and Darya as gatekeepers hoarding knowledge that should be democratized — or rather, monetized. She wants to build a chain of apothecary stalls in every village on the trade road.

npc.backstory:
Oleg controls the only mill in Oakhollow. Every grain of wheat that becomes flour passes through his stones, and he takes his cut — a generous one. He inherited the mill from his father and considers it his birthright to profit from every loaf of bread the village eats.

He coordinates with Lira on grain prices: she controls what comes in from the trade routes, he controls what gets processed locally. Together, they have quietly raised the price of flour by a third over two years. Most villagers blame bad harvests.

He is not cruel, exactly — he simply sees the world in ledgers and margins. Petra's struggles are a line item. Nadia's cooperative idea is a threat to his monopoly. Victor's loans are a useful tool. His wife left him five years ago, tired of living with a man who counted coins in his sleep.

npc.backstory:
Ivan has dug graves behind the Chapel of the Dawn for forty years. He started as a young man with strong arms and no better prospects, and somewhere along the way, the dead became better company than the living.

He claims to hear the dead whispering to him from their graves. Brother Aldric calls this heresy. Sister Elara calls it grief. Ivan calls it Tuesday. Whether his gift is real or the product of decades of solitude is a matter of debate, but his knowledge of the village's history is undeniable — he has buried three generations and remembers every name on every stone.

He knows things that should have been forgotten. He knows where the old lord's treasure was buried. He knows which family lines are not what they claim. He knows the real reason the forest is corrupted, because the dead told him, and no one alive wants to hear it.
npc.name: Ivan the Gravedigger
goals: tend the graves properly
find someone willing to listen to what the dead say
record the village's true history before he joins the buried

npc.backstory:
Nadia keeps twelve hives on the edge of Millfield Farm, producing honey that she sells at the market. She is young, fierce, and angry about the way Victor and Oleg squeeze the village's farmers and workers.

She has been organizing quietly — talking to Petra, to Bran, to other farmhands — about forming a workers' cooperative that would buy grain directly and mill it communally, cutting out Oleg's monopoly. Victor sees her as a threat to the entire economic order he has built. Oleg wants her gone.

She is pregnant, a fact she has shared only with Ilse the midwife. The father is a traveling merchant who passed through two months ago and will not return. She is not ashamed, but she knows the village will talk. She plans to raise the child alone, like Petra, but with a community behind her instead of debt.

Her bees are her metaphor: small things working together can build something sweet.
npc.name: Nadia the Beekeeper
goals: establish a workers' cooperative
break Victor and Oleg's economic stranglehold
raise her child in a fairer village
convince the council to regulate moneylending

npc_actions.dialogue: Hey there, I heard you've got info on the sickness! I'm Bran, farmhand around here. What's your story?
npc_actions.reasoning: The village needs answers, and I'm excited to take on the role of an investigator. My curiosity is too strong to resist.

npc_actions.dialogue: I'll need to gather some supplies before venturing into the forest.
npc_actions.reasoning: A mysterious figure with information about the sickness has approached the village, and there's a hidden cave near the forest edge. I must explore this to uncover more secrets of Oakhollow.

npc_actions.dialogue: I'll take a look around this cave and see what secrets it holds.
npc_actions.reasoning: I can't resist a good mystery, and the wardens' monopoly on forest resources makes me even more curious about what's going on in there.

npc_actions.dialogue: I'll have to see what's going on in that cave.
npc_actions.reasoning: The village's economy and my control over it are at stake. I need to know what's happening in that cave.

npc_actions.dialogue: Nadia, have you heard about the mysterious figure who came to town? I think they might have information about the sickness!
npc_actions.reasoning: Yara is excited and full of curiosity, and Nadia is a close friend who might share her enthusiasm.

npc_actions.dialogue: Elder Marta, I heard a rumor about a mysterious figure who claimed to know something about the sickness. Might be worth checking out!
npc_actions.reasoning: I can't resist spreading and gathering information. Elder Marta is a great source and recipient of gossip.

npc_actions.dialogue: Have you heard the latest news about the mysterious figure who arrived in town? I wonder if they have any information about the sickness.
npc_actions.reasoning: I want to gather more information about the mysterious figure and see if it can be used to my advantage. Lira seems to have her fingers on the pulse of the village.

interactions.summary: Magda and Rolf exchange pleasantries and discuss the current state of the village, with Rolf mentioning the northern army and Magda expressing concern about the sickness.
Thessa the Bard and Finn the Troublemaker share a light-hearted conversation, discussing rumors and the ongoing mystery in the village.
Old Tom tells Senna a story about his youth, while Senna listens intently and shares a bit about her current struggles.
Elder Marta and Zev the Beggar have a brief but friendly conversation, discussing the recent developments in the village and the spreading sickness.
Torvin the Smith and Vasek the Carpenter meet at Ironheart Smithy to discuss their ongoing secret project. They share information and express excitement about their progress.
Lira the Merchant and Katya the Apothecary exchange pleasantries and discuss recent events in the village, touching briefly on the spreading sickness.
Hilde the Weaver and Anya the Baker meet at the village market and exchange pleasantries about their trades and the village's recent concerns.
Bran and Petra discuss the recent sickness and how to help the village. Petra shares some concerns about her debt, and Bran offers words of encouragement.
Brother Aldric and Ilse the Midwife share concerns about Elara's healing powers and the recent sickness in the village. They discuss the potential connection between Elara's work and the dark ritual.
Goran the Hunter confronts Korin the Poacher over the recent decline in deer populations and the disturbances in the forest. The argument escalates into a hostile confrontation.

active_scenarios.title: The Spreading Sickness
active_scenarios.description: A mysterious illness begins affecting villagers, causing fear and suspicion to spread faster than the disease itself.
active_scenarios.narrative_update: Katya and Sister Elara have discovered a dark ritual being conducted in the forest, believed to be the source of the sickness. The village must now decide how to confront the perpetrators and prevent the sickness from spreading further.

events.description: A sudden and unexpected downpour in the evening causes the river near Oakhollow Mill to swell, disrupting the milling process and leading to a temporary halt in flour production.

During the evening at the Rusty Flagon, a heated argument breaks out between two groups of villagers over the best course of action regarding the sickness, escalating into a brief but tense confrontation.
The healer reveals the true cause of the sickness, a curse tied to an ancient artifact.
The village comes together to perform a cleansing ritual, purifying the artifact and the affected areas.

npc_actions.dialogue: Hey there, Rolf! Heard anything interesting about the Whisperers lately?
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm excited and eager to gather more information about the Whisperers, and Rolf is often in the know due to his former soldier days.

npc_actions.dialogue: Hey Finn, heard any good gossip lately? Something juicy about the northern army would be perfect!
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm feeling excited and want to spread or gather some juicy rumors. Finn always has something interesting to share.
With the morning rush approaching, it's time to start preparing the tavern's breakfast menu to ensure everyone is fed and ready for the day ahead.

npc_actions.dialogue: Hey Thessa, heard any good gossip lately? I've got a few stories of my own to share!
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm in the mood for some lively chatter and I know Thessa always has the juiciest tales.

npc_actions.dialogue: Good morning, Anya! I've heard about your grandmother's special bread. Perhaps we could trade some of your loaves for some spices and rare ingredients from beyond the village?
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm excited about expanding my trade network and believe that a mutually beneficial deal with Anya could be the start of a profitable relationship.

npc_actions.dialogue: Hey Petra! You look like you've got a lot on your mind. Want to grab a bite to eat and chat over breakfast?
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm feeling excited and Petra is someone I trust and get along with well. A chat over breakfast sounds like a good way to start the day and maybe help her out with her worries.

npc_actions.dialogue: I need to understand the source of the dark energy troubling our village.
npc_actions.reasoning: I feel a duty to uncover the truth behind the mysterious merchant, despite the danger and Brother Aldric's hostility.
The recent disturbances and the fear in the air call for extra vigilance. I must ensure the safety of the village by patrolling the Whispering Woods.
Despite my fear, my duty as the captain demands that I maintain vigilance over the village. The threat of the northern menace is ever-present, and I must lead by example.

npc_actions.dialogue: Ilse, I've been pondering our discussion about Elara's powers and the recent events in our village. I believe we must delve deeper into the origins of her abilities and their connection to the sickness.
npc_actions.reasoning: I need to ensure Ilse remains aligned with my concerns about Sister Elara. Her insights as a midwife are invaluable.

npc_actions.dialogue: Sister Elara, I've been thinking about the villagers' sickness and how we can better manage the healing process. Could we perhaps find a way to distribute the workload more evenly?
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm excited about the prospect of helping Sister Elara, who is overwhelmed with the number of sick villagers. I want to support her and find a solution that benefits everyone.

The recent disturbances and the pack of wolves spotted near the forest edge have me curious about the hidden cave. Perhaps there's something there that could help me gain an edge over Goran and the wardens.

npc_actions.dialogue: I've heard some rather interesting things about the recent thefts. It seems someone is targeting merchants specifically.
npc_actions.reasoning: I am feeling particularly irate this morning, and gossiping is a subtle way to sow discord and assert my influence.

interactions.summary: Thessa the Bard and Rolf the Drunk share a conversation about the recent events in the village, including the neutralization of the source of the Spreading Sickness.
Finn the Troublemaker buys Old Tom a drink and listens to his tales of adventure and wisdom, showing a bit of interest despite his usual demeanor.
Senna and Magda chat warmly about the recent decrease in sickness cases, and Magda offers Senna a hearty meal.

Elder Marta and Zev the Beggar meet in the village square to discuss the recent developments regarding the spreading sickness. They exchange information and Elder Marta offers Zev some food and shelter for the winter.
Torvin the Smith and Vasek the Carpenter meet to discuss their secret project and the progress they are making towards completing it. They are both excited and supportive of each other's efforts.
Lira the Merchant and Katya the Apothecary exchange pleasantries and share news about the village's recent success in combating the mysterious illness.
Anya the Baker and Victor the Moneylender share a brief conversation about the village's market and the recent improvements in the village's health situation.
Petra the Farmer and Bran the Farmhand have a friendly chat about the recent improvements in the village's health situation.
Brother Aldric and Ilse the Midwife discuss the recent progress in combating the spreading sickness and the spiritual implications for the chapel.
Korin and Goran engage in a heated argument about the decline of deer population and the need to control the forest resources, each blaming the other for the problem.

active_scenarios.title: The Spreading Sickness
active_scenarios.description: A mysterious illness begins affecting villagers, causing fear and suspicion to spread faster than the disease itself.
active_scenarios.narrative_update: The village's efforts to combat the mysterious illness begin to bear fruit as the source of the sickness is neutralized, and a sense of relief and hope spreads among the villagers.

events.description: A farmer's child went missing near the forest edge. Search parties are being organized.
The village well water tastes strange. Some fear it has been poisoned, others blame the recent rains.
The healer announces the source of the sickness and the steps taken to address it.
Villagers work together to clean and disinfect their homes and streets.
The village elder praises the community's resilience and unity.

npc_actions.dialogue: Hey Rolf, I hear the well water tastes off. Anyone's seen anything strange around there?
npc_actions.reasoning: I feel excited and want to gather more information about the recent events in the village, especially about the mysterious well water issue.

npc_actions.dialogue: Oi, Finn! Got any juicy rumors about the missing kid or the funny-tasting well water?
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm feeling excited and curious about the recent events in the village, and Finn always has something interesting to say.

npc_actions.dialogue: Senna, I heard you're looking for work. Why don't you help me in the kitchen today? I've got plenty to do and could use an extra pair of hands.
npc_actions.reasoning: Senna needs guidance and support, and helping her with a job can provide her with a sense of purpose and safety.

npc_actions.dialogue: Hey Magda, I heard about the missing farmer's kid. Any news about that?
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm excited and curious about the missing child, and Magda seems like someone who might have inside information.

npc_actions.dialogue: Hey there Thessa, I heard the water in the well tastes funny. Have you heard anything about it?
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm feeling excited and curious, so I want to share my thoughts with someone who might know more. Thessa is a good listener and knows a lot about what's happening around here.

npc_actions.dialogue: Hey Thessa, heard a kid went missing in the woods. Any songs about lost children?
npc_actions.reasoning: I love stirring up gossip and playing the trickster, and Thessa is always up for a good story.

npc_actions.dialogue: I'll check the well for any signs of tampering or contamination.
npc_actions.reasoning: The water tasting strange is a matter of concern for the village's health. As an elder who prioritizes safety, it's my duty to look into this and ensure the well remains safe.
Today's excitement and the recent positive interactions with Vasek make me eager to put in extra effort on my craft. Perhaps today is the day I can make progress on the masterwork blade.

npc_actions.dialogue: Anya, have you considered selling some of your finest loaves to the nearby towns? I could help you set up a distribution network.
npc_actions.reasoning: Today's a good day to expand my trade network and help Anya increase her profits. The village well's water tasting strange could impact our supplies, so it's best to act now while things are still stable.

npc_actions.dialogue: Hey, have you seen that guy lurking around? Something's not right.
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm curious about the mysterious figure and want to know more about them.
I'm feeling excited and eager to work today. Crafting a new batch of pastries will not only keep me busy but also bring joy to those who visit the bakery.

npc_actions.dialogue: I need to examine the village well closely to determine if the water has truly been tainted.
npc_actions.reasoning: I am driven by the curiosity and need to protect my village. The strange taste of the well water worries me, and I must act to safeguard the health of the villagers.
As a hunter, my duty is to protect the village and its surroundings. With recent events and the strange occurrences in the woods, I need to keep a close watch.

npc_actions.dialogue: I'll head into the woods to see if I can find any clues about the missing child.
npc_actions.reasoning: My excitement for adventure is too strong to ignore, and the disappearance of the farmer's child is a mystery that needs solving. As a herbalist, I might find something useful in the woods.

npc_actions.dialogue: Lira, have you heard anything new about the missing child near the forest? It's quite a mystery, isn't it?
npc_actions.reasoning: I can't resist a good mystery and Lira often has the latest news. This is a chance to gather information and perhaps find a clue.
Despite my fear, the village needs to be vigilant. I can't let my personal feelings affect the safety of the villagers.

npc_actions.dialogue: Ilse, have you noticed any strange occurrences lately? I've been feeling restless and eager to discuss the village's spiritual well-being.
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm excited and want to engage Ilse in a discussion about the village's spiritual health, especially concerning the recent events and Sister Elara's powers.

npc_actions.dialogue: Good morning, Torvin. How's the progress on our secret project?
npc_actions.reasoning: I am excited about the secret project and want to discuss it with Torvin.

Today, I'll focus on the routine work needed to maintain the farm and keep it running smoothly. The debt to Victor weighs heavily on my mind, but I know that by putting in the hours and tending to the land, I can make a small dent in it.

npc_actions.dialogue: Good morning, Sister Elara! I've been thinking about the chapel's recent efforts to heal the sick. I wanted to see if there's anything I could help with, perhaps combining my knowledge of childbirth and sickness with your skills as a healer.
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm eager to contribute to the chapel's healing work and to deepen my connection with Sister Elara. I believe my insights as a midwife could complement her healing practices.

npc_actions.dialogue: I'll check out that hidden cave before anyone else can.
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm curious about what's in that cave, and it could be a good spot to set up a trap or find some valuable goods.

npc_actions.dialogue: I've heard some interesting rumors about the strange taste in the village well. Could be a curse or even poisoning, but I think it's just the rain affecting the water's flavor.
npc_actions.reasoning: I need to keep Lira close and informed, while subtly suggesting danger to make her rely on me.

npc_actions.dialogue: Nadia, have you heard anything new about the missing child? I can't stop thinking about it.
npc_actions.reasoning: Yara's curiosity and empathy get the best of her. She wants to share her concerns and seek comfort from a trusted friend.

npc_actions.dialogue: Elder Marta, have you heard about the strange taste in the well water? Some folks are getting real nervous!
npc_actions.reasoning: I'm excited to share new information and Elder Marta is a good source for updates.

npc_actions.dialogue: Lira, have you heard about the strange taste in the village well? It's quite the talk of the town!
npc_actions.reasoning: I need to keep Lira engaged and curious, using the mystery to my advantage. Gossiping will help me manipulate the situation.

interactions.summary: Magda the Cook and Thessa the Bard exchange pleasantries over the recent improvement in the village's health situation.
Senna the Orphan and Rolf the Drunk share a casual conversation at The Rusty Flagon, discussing recent village news.
Old Tom and Finn the Troublemaker chat at The Rusty Flagon, with Old Tom enthusiastically sharing tales from his past while Finn listens, albeit with a hint of skepticism.
Zev the Beggar and Elder Marta meet in the Village Square to share information about the recent events in the village and discuss preparations for winter.
Torvin and Vasek meet at Ironheart Smithy to discuss the progress of their secret project. They share their excitement about the project and their mutual goals.
Lira the Merchant and Anya the Baker exchange pleasantries and discuss the village's recovery from the recent sickness.

Hilde the Weaver and Katya the Apothecary exchange pleasantries and discuss recent village happenings, particularly focusing on the recovery from the spreading sickness and the improvements in communal hygiene practices.

Petra the Farmer and Yara the Stablehand meet at Millfield Farm and have a friendly chat. Petra thanks Yara for her help with the horses, and Yara inquires about Petra's health and the farm's progress.

Sister Elara and Ilse the Midwife discuss the recent improvement in the village's health after the well contamination was resolved. Ilse is excited to share that she has discovered some family secrets that might help in the chapel's spiritual protection efforts.
Korin and Darya meet in Whispering Woods and have a friendly conversation about their recent findings and goals.

active_scenarios.title: The Spreading Sickness
active_scenarios.description: A mysterious illness begins affecting villagers, causing fear and suspicion to spread faster than the disease itself.
active_scenarios.narrative_update: The village's healer, after days of relentless study, identifies the source of the sickness as a contaminated well. Once the well is sealed and the poisoned water removed, the villagers begin to recover. The community rallies together, using the experience to improve their hygiene and communal health practices.
